recipe.step: Heat olive oil in a large pan over medium-high. Add onion, cook until soft — 5 min. Add garlic, cook 1 min.
Add ground beef. Break up with a spoon and cook until no pink remains — 8 min. Drain excess fat.
Pour in pasta sauce and milk. Add Italian seasoning. Stir and simmer on low, partially covered — 20 min.
Meanwhile, cook spaghetti in heavily salted boiling water until al dente — 9 min. Reserve ½ cup pasta water before draining.
Toss pasta with sauce. If too thick, add a splash of pasta water. Plate and top with Parmesan.
Cook fettuccine in heavily salted boiling water until al dente — 10 min. Reserve 1 cup pasta water. Drain and set aside.
Season chicken with salt and pepper. Melt 2 tbsp butter in a large pan over medium-high. Cook chicken 5 min per side until golden and cooked through. Transfer to a board, rest 5 min, then slice.
In the same pan over medium, melt remaining 2 tbsp butter. Add garlic, cook 1 min. Pour in cream, bring to a gentle simmer.
Add Parmesan and nutmeg. Stir until sauce thickens — 3 min. Season with salt and pepper.
Add pasta to the sauce. Toss to coat, adding pasta water a splash at a time to reach a silky consistency. Top with chicken.
Cook pasta in heavily salted boiling water until al dente — 10 min. Reserve ½ cup pasta water. Drain.
In a large pan over medium-high, cook sausage, breaking it up — 8 min until browned. Transfer to a plate.
Add olive oil to the same pan. Cook zucchini until golden — 4 min. Add garlic and chili flakes — 1 min.
Add tomatoes and olives. Simmer 15 min, breaking up tomatoes. Return sausage for the last 5 min.
Toss pasta with sauce. Add pasta water as needed. Finish with Parmesan.
Heat olive oil in a large pan over medium-high. Cook onion 5 min until soft. Add garlic 1 min.
Add ground beef. Break up and cook until browned — 8 min. Drain fat.
Add pasta sauce and Italian seasoning. Simmer 15 min on medium-low.
Cook tortellini in salted boiling water per package (usually 3 min). Drain.
Combine tortellini with sauce. Plate and top generously with Parmesan.
Cook tortellini in heavily salted boiling water per package — about 3 min. Reserve ¼ cup pasta water before draining.
In a large pan over medium, melt butter. Add garlic and cook until fragrant and just starting to turn golden — 2 min. Don't let it burn.
Add drained tortellini to the pan. Toss to coat in garlic butter — 1 min.
Add Parmesan and a splash of pasta water. Toss until cheesy and glossy. Crack lots of black pepper over the top.
Preheat oven to 400°F. Toss cherry tomatoes with 2 tbsp olive oil, salt and pepper on a baking sheet. Roast until burst and caramelized — 20 min.
Cook pasta in heavily salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve ½ cup pasta water. Drain.
In a large pan over medium, heat remaining olive oil. Add garlic and chili flakes, cook until garlic is golden — 2 min.
Add roasted tomatoes and pasta. Toss together, adding pasta water as needed. Stir in butter and most of the basil.
Divide into bowls. Place a ball of burrata on top of each. Break it open at the table. Finish with basil and a drizzle of olive oil.
Preheat oven to 375°F. Cook lasagna noodles in salted boiling water until al dente — 9 min. Drain and lay flat on an oiled baking sheet.
Mix ricotta with egg, Parmesan and 1 tsp Italian seasoning in a bowl. Season with salt and pepper.
In a 9×13 baking dish: spread a thin layer of pizza sauce, then noodles, ricotta mixture, mozzarella, pepperoni. Repeat twice, finishing with sauce and mozzarella.
Cover with foil. Bake 35 min. Remove foil, bake until bubbly and golden — 10 min more. Rest 10 min before cutting.
Prepare pizza kit per instructions alongside lasagna.
Preheat oven to 375°F. Cook pasta in salted boiling water until just under al dente — 7 min. It will finish in the oven. Drain.
In a large pan over medium-high, brown sausage, breaking it up — 8 min. Drain fat. Add garlic 1 min. Add pasta sauce, simmer 10 min.
Mix ricotta, egg and half the Parmesan in a bowl. Season with salt and pepper.
In a 9×13 dish: mix pasta with meat sauce. Dollop ricotta mixture throughout. Top with mozzarella and remaining Parmesan.
Bake until bubbling and golden on top — 25–30 min. Rest 5 min before serving.
Cook gnocchi in a large pot of salted boiling water until they float to the surface — 2–3 min. Do in two batches if needed. Drain well.
In a wide pan over medium, melt butter. Swirl constantly as it melts. Continue cooking until it turns golden and smells nutty — 4–5 min. Watch closely, it can burn quickly.
Add sage leaves — they'll crisp in 30 sec. Add garlic — cook 30 sec.
Add drained gnocchi to the pan. Toss to coat. Season with salt and lots of black pepper.
Plate and finish generously with Parmesan.
Cook pasta in salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve ½ cup pasta water. Drain.
Season chicken with Italian seasoning, salt and pepper. Sear in olive oil over medium-high — 5 min per side until golden and cooked through. Transfer to board, rest 5 min, slice.
In the same pan, add butter and garlic. Cook 1 min. Add sun-dried tomatoes, cook 2 min.
Pour in broth and cream. Bring to a simmer, cook until slightly thickened — 3 min. Add Parmesan, stir until melted.
Add pasta (and spinach if using) to the sauce. Toss to combine. Top with sliced chicken.
Cook penne in heavily salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve ½ cup pasta water. Drain.
In a large pan over medium-high, cook sausage, breaking it up — 8 min until well browned. Transfer to a plate.
In the same pan over medium, add olive oil. Add garlic and chili flakes — cook until garlic is golden, not burnt — 2 min.
Add crushed tomatoes. Season with salt. Simmer until thickened — 15 min.
Return sausage to the sauce — 5 min. Toss with penne, adding pasta water as needed. Finish with basil and Parmesan.
Season chicken thighs well with paprika, garlic powder, salt and pepper. Heat olive oil in a large oven-safe pan or Dutch oven over medium-high.
Sear chicken skin-side down until deep golden — 5 min. Flip, cook 3 min. Remove to a plate.
Pour off most of the fat, leaving about 1 tbsp. Add garlic, cook 1 min. Add rice and stir to coat in the fat — 1 min.
Pour in broth. Bring to a boil. Nestle chicken on top, skin-side up. Cover tightly, reduce to low. Cook until rice is tender and chicken is cooked through — 20 min.
Microwave frozen veg with butter and a splash of water, covered, for 4 min. Serve alongside.
Marinate chicken in yogurt, garlic, ginger, garam masala and salt for at least 30 min (overnight is better). Mix well to coat every piece.
Melt butter in a large pan over medium-high. Add onion, cook until golden — 8 min.
Add marinated chicken. Cook until browned on all sides — 6 min. Don't worry about cooking through yet.
Pour in butter chicken sauce. Reduce to medium-low. Cover and simmer until chicken is fully cooked and sauce is thick — 20 min. Stir in cream for the last 3 min.
Cook basmati rice using half coconut milk and half water (total liquid = 2¼ cups for 1½ cups rice). Bring to boil, cover, simmer 15 min on lowest heat. Rest 5 min off heat.
Soak rice noodles in warm water until pliable — 10 min. Drain and set aside. (Or follow package instructions.)
Season chicken with salt and pepper. Melt butter in a large pan over medium-high. Cook chicken in a single layer — 3 min per side until golden and cooked through. Transfer to a plate.
Add garlic to the same pan — 1 min. Add frozen veg, stir-fry until hot and starting to char — 4 min.
Add noodles, soy sauce, lemon juice and zest. Toss everything together — 2 min. Drizzle sesame oil.
Slice chicken and lay on top. Finish with sesame seeds and green onion if using.
Cook rice: rinse, add to pot with 2¼ cups water. Bring to boil, cover, cook on lowest heat 15 min. Rest 5 min off heat.
Toss chicken slices with cornstarch, salt and pepper.
Heat sesame oil and butter in a wok or large pan over high heat until smoking. Add chicken — stir-fry until cooked through and golden — 4 min. Remove to a plate.
Add garlic and ginger to the hot pan — 30 sec. Add vegetables, stir-fry over high heat until tender-crisp — 4 min.
Return chicken. Add soy sauce and oyster sauce. Toss together — 1 min. Serve over rice.
Slice chicken into thin strips. Toss with fajita seasoning and 1 tbsp olive oil. Let sit 10 min while you prep the veg.
Heat a cast iron or heavy pan over high heat until very hot. Add 1 tbsp olive oil. Cook chicken strips in a single layer — 3 min per side until charred and cooked through. Transfer to a plate.
Add remaining oil to the hot pan. Add peppers and onion. Cook over high heat, stirring occasionally, until softened and slightly charred — 6 min. Season with salt.
Warm tortillas directly over a gas burner 10 sec per side, or wrap in a damp paper towel and microwave 45 sec.
Serve everything family-style — chicken, peppers, tortillas, sour cream, cheese, salsa, and lime wedges on the table.
Marinate chicken in yogurt, lemon juice, oregano, garlic, salt and pepper for at least 30 min.
Preheat oven to 400°F. Heat olive oil in an oven-safe pan over medium-high. Sear chicken 3 min per side until golden. Transfer to oven. Roast until cooked through — 20 min.
Meanwhile, cook rice in chicken broth — bring to boil, cover, simmer on low 15 min. Rest 5 min.
Warm pita in the oven for the last 3 min of the chicken cooking.
Slice chicken. Arrange over rice with tomatoes, cucumber and olives. Crumble feta over top. Drizzle with olive oil and a squeeze of lemon.

pantry.name: Celery sticks and blue cheese or ranch

recipe.step: Preheat oven to 250°F. Pat wings completely dry with paper towels — this is the key to crispy skin. Toss with baking powder, garlic powder, onion powder and salt.
Place wings on a rack set over a baking sheet. Bake at 250°F for 30 min — this renders the fat.
Increase oven to 425°F. Continue baking until deep golden and crispy, flipping once — 40–45 min.
While wings finish, spread fries on a separate baking sheet. Bake per package directions (usually 20–25 min at 425°F).
Melt butter in a small pan. Add hot sauce. Toss wings in the sauce immediately before serving.
Preheat oven to 425°F. Cook rice: 1½ cups rice to 2¼ cups water, bring to boil, cover, simmer 15 min.
Season chicken with paprika, garlic powder, salt and pepper. Spread on one baking sheet, veg on another tray with olive oil and seasoning. Roast both at 425°F — chicken 22 min, veg 20 min.
Add fries to a third baking sheet. Bake alongside everything at 425°F — 20–22 min until very crispy.
Make gravy per packet directions — keep hot on the stove.
For poutine: pile hot fries in a bowl, scatter cheese curds, pour hot gravy over immediately. The heat melts the curds. Serve poutine alongside power bowls of rice, chicken and veg.
Marinate chicken in yogurt, shawarma spice, lemon juice, olive oil, salt and pepper — at least 1 hour, ideally overnight.
Cook basmati rice in 2¼ cups salted water — bring to boil, cover, simmer 15 min on low. Rest 5 min off heat.
Heat a large pan or grill over high heat. Cook chicken 6–7 min per side until charred and cooked through. Cook onions in the same pan — 5 min until softened and slightly charred.
Rest chicken 5 min. Slice thin against the grain.
Warm pita in the oven at 350°F for 3 min or directly on the gas burner. Serve chicken over rice with pita, garlic sauce, pickles and onions.
In a bowl, whisk together honey, soy sauce, garlic and ketchup.
Season chicken with salt and pepper. Melt butter in a large pan over medium-high. Sear chicken — 3 min per side until golden.
Pour sauce over chicken. Reduce to medium-low, cover and cook until chicken is cooked through — 15 min, turning once.
Remove chicken. Mix cornstarch with 2 tbsp cold water, stir into the sauce in the pan. Cook on medium until thickened — 2 min. Return chicken.
Cook rice: 1½ cups rice with 2¼ cups water, bring to boil, cover, simmer 15 min. Rest 5 min. Serve chicken over rice, topped with green onions.
Preheat oven to 425°F. Press one pie crust into a 9-inch pie dish.
Melt butter in a large pan over medium. Cook onion until soft — 5 min. Add garlic — 1 min. Sprinkle flour over and stir to coat — cook 2 min.
Gradually pour in broth and milk, whisking constantly until smooth. Add thyme. Simmer until thickened — 5 min.
Stir in chicken and frozen veg. Season generously with salt and pepper. Pour into the pie crust.
Top with second crust, crimp edges, cut 4–5 steam vents. Bake until crust is golden — 30–35 min. Rest 10 min before cutting.
Boil potatoes in salted water until tender — 15 min. Drain, toss with olive oil, salt and pepper. Set aside.
Pound chicken breasts between plastic wrap to ½-inch thickness using a rolling pin or the bottom of a pan.
Set up three shallow bowls: flour seasoned with salt and pepper, beaten eggs, panko mixed with garlic powder and paprika.
Coat each chicken piece: flour → shake off excess → egg → panko. Press the breadcrumbs firmly to adhere.
Heat butter and olive oil in a large pan over medium-high. Fry schnitzel until golden — 3–4 min per side. Work in batches, don't crowd the pan. Serve with potatoes and lemon.
Cook rice: rinse well, add to pot with 2¼ cups water. Bring to boil, cover, cook on lowest heat 15 min. Rest 5 min.
Whisk together soy sauce, mirin, sugar, garlic and ginger in a small bowl.
Heat sesame oil in a large pan over medium-high. Add chicken — cook 5 min per side until cooked through and golden.
Pour teriyaki sauce over the chicken. It will bubble up. Cook until sauce thickens and coats the chicken — 3–4 min, turning to glaze.
Transfer chicken to a board. Mix cornstarch with 1 tbsp water, stir into any remaining sauce and cook 1 min to thicken. Slice chicken and serve over rice. Drizzle with extra sauce and garnish.
Preheat oven to 375°F. Mix garlic powder, rosemary, thyme, salt and generous pepper. Pat pork loin dry and rub all over with olive oil, then the spice mix.
Heat an oven-safe pan over high heat. Sear the pork on all sides until deeply browned — 2 min per side, about 8 min total.
Toss potatoes, carrots and whole garlic cloves with olive oil, salt and pepper. Scatter around the pork in the pan.
Roast until a meat thermometer reads 145°F in the centre of the pork — 45–55 min. Rest on a board for 10 min before slicing.
While pork rests, return pan to medium-high heat. Add butter to the vegetable drippings. Toss veg to glaze.
If using wooden skewers, soak in water 30 min to prevent burning. Marinate pork cubes in olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, oregano, cumin, paprika, salt and pepper — 20 min minimum.
Cook rice: 1½ cups rice with 2¼ cups salted water. Bring to boil, cover, simmer 15 min. Rest 5 min.
Thread pork, peppers and onion alternately onto skewers.
Grill or cook under broiler on high — 10–12 min total, turning every 3 min, until pork is cooked through and slightly charred.
Serve skewers over rice with a simple salad dressed with lemon and olive oil.
Slice pork thin or cut into small chunks. Toss with taco seasoning and olive oil.
Cook pork in a hot pan over medium-high, stirring occasionally, until cooked through and slightly crispy on the edges — 8–10 min.
Make the slaw: toss coleslaw mix with apple cider vinegar, honey, salt and a squeeze of lime.
Warm tortillas in a dry pan 30 sec per side, or wrap in foil and warm in oven at 300°F for 10 min.
Serve everything family style — pork, slaw, sour cream, cheese, salsa and lime on the table.
Mix brown sugar, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, cumin and salt. Rub all over pork shoulder.
Place pork in slow cooker. Pour ½ cup BBQ sauce over. Cook on LOW 8–10 hours or HIGH 5–6 hours until falling apart.
Transfer pork to a large bowl. Discard excess liquid. Shred with two forks — it should fall apart easily. Toss with remaining BBQ sauce.
Make slaw: toss coleslaw mix with mayo, apple cider vinegar, a pinch of sugar, salt and pepper.
Toast buns cut-side down in a dry pan 1 min. Load with pulled pork and slaw.
Boil potatoes in salted water until just tender — 12 min. Drain.
Season pork chops generously with salt, pepper, thyme and rosemary. Heat olive oil in a large oven-safe pan over high heat.
Sear chops — 3 min per side until deep golden. Transfer to a plate.
Reduce to medium. Add butter, onion and apple to the pan. Cook 5 min until softened. Stir in brown sugar and Dijon. Add broth, scraping up any browned bits.
Return pork chops and potatoes to the pan. Cover and cook on medium-low until chops are cooked through — 10 min. Rest 3 min.
Cook rice per package. Mix ketchup, vinegar, soy sauce, brown sugar and 3 tbsp pineapple juice in a bowl. Set aside.
Toss pork cubes in beaten egg, then coat well in cornstarch. Shake off excess.
Heat 2 cups oil in a wok or deep pan to 375°F. Fry pork in batches — 3–4 min per batch until golden and crispy. Drain on paper towels.
Pour off all but 1 tbsp oil. Over high heat, stir-fry peppers and onion — 3 min. Add pineapple — 1 min.
Pour sweet and sour sauce into the pan. Bring to a simmer until slightly thickened — 2 min. Add pork and toss to coat. Serve immediately over rice.
Preheat oven to 425°F. Spread fries on a baking sheet. Bake until golden and crispy — 20–25 min, flipping halfway.

Mix ground beef gently with Worcestershire, garlic powder, salt and pepper. Divide into 4 portions. Form into patties slightly wider than the buns — they'll shrink. Press a dimple in the centre of each.
Heat a cast iron pan or grill over high heat. Cook burgers — 3–4 min per side for medium, 4–5 min for well done. Don't press with a spatula.
Add cheese slice in the last minute. Cover with a lid to melt.
Toast buns cut-side down in the pan — 1 min. Build burgers and serve with fries.

pantry.name: Special sauce (mayo, ketchup, pickle relish mixed)

recipe.step: Preheat oven to 425°F. Cook fries per package — 20–25 min until very crispy.
Divide beef into 8 loose balls of about 3 oz each — do not compress them. Season tops generously with salt.

Heat cast iron pan over HIGH heat until smoking — this is key. Add butter. Place 2 beef balls in the pan. Immediately smash each flat with a heavy spatula and some parchment paper — press hard to ¼ inch thick.
Cook until edges are brown and crusty — 90 sec. Flip once, add cheese, cook 60 sec more. The crust should be dark and lacey. Repeat for all patties.
Toast buns cut-side down in the pan — 30 sec. Stack two patties per bun. Add special sauce, pickles, onion and lettuce.
Preheat oven to 325°F. Pat chuck roast dry. Season very generously on all sides with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a Dutch oven over high heat. Sear roast — 3–4 min per side until deeply browned all over. This step builds flavour — don't rush it.
Add onion and garlic to the pot. Cook 2 min. Add tomato paste, stir around the meat. Pour in broth and wine. Add thyme, rosemary and Worcestershire.
Cover and roast in the oven until very tender and falling apart — 2.5–3 hours. Add potatoes and carrots in the last 1 hour.
Remove roast to a board. Shred or slice. Taste the braising liquid and season. Serve with veg and spoonfuls of the rich braising juice.
Mix ground beef with garlic powder, onion powder, oregano, cayenne, salt and pepper. Form into a tightly packed loaf shape. Bake at 375°F on a rack — 40 min until firm and cooked through.

Make the sauce: beat evaporated milk with an electric mixer or whisk until slightly thickened — 2 min. Gradually beat in sugar, vinegar and garlic powder. Sauce should be pourable but coat a spoon. Refrigerate until needed.
Rest the meat 10 min. Slice thin.
Warm pitas or tortillas in a dry pan 30 sec per side.
Set up toppings family style: meat, tomato, onion, cheese, lettuce. Drizzle donair sauce generously over everything in the wrap.

pantry.name: Taco shells (hard) or small flour tortillas

recipe.step: Brown ground beef in a pan over medium-high, breaking it up — 8 min. Drain excess fat.
Add taco seasoning and ⅓ cup water. Stir to combine. Simmer on medium until liquid is absorbed — 3 min.
Warm taco shells in oven at 350°F for 5 min, or tortillas in a dry pan 30 sec per side.
Set up everything family style: meat, shells, cheese, lettuce, tomato, sour cream, salsa and lime on the table.
Brown ground beef with taco seasoning — 8 min. Drain fat, add a splash of water, simmer 3 min. Keep warm.
Toss sliced steak or chicken with fajita seasoning and 1 tbsp oil.
Cook steak/chicken in a hot pan over high heat — 3 min per side until charred. Remove to a board, rest and slice.
In the same pan, cook peppers and onion with olive oil over high heat — 5 min until charred and softened.
Warm tortillas. Serve everything DIY-style on the table.
Season beef with salt and pepper. Toss with flour to coat. Heat olive oil in a large pan over high heat. Brown beef in batches — don't crowd the pan — 3 min per side. You want a deep crust.
Transfer beef to slow cooker. Add all vegetables, garlic, broth, tomato paste, Worcestershire, thyme and bay leaves.
Cook on LOW 8 hours or HIGH 4–5 hours. Beef should be very tender and falling apart.
Remove bay leaves. Taste and adjust seasoning. For a thicker stew, mix 2 tbsp cornstarch with 2 tbsp cold water and stir in. Cook on HIGH 15 min.
Warm bread in oven at 350°F for 10 min. Serve stew in deep bowls with bread for dipping.
Cook rice: rinse, combine with 2¼ cups water. Bring to boil, cover, simmer 15 min on lowest heat. Rest 5 min.
In a small bowl, whisk soy sauce, brown sugar, sesame oil, garlic, ginger and gochujang.
Heat a large pan over medium-high. Add butter and sesame oil. Cook ground beef, breaking up, until browned — 8 min. Drain excess fat.
Pour sauce over beef. Stir to combine. If you want a thicker sauce, mix cornstarch with 1 tbsp water and add now. Cook 2 min until glossy.
Serve beef over rice. Top with green onions and sesame seeds. Add a fried egg on top if desired.

fridge.name: Beef sirloin or chuck, sliced thin

recipe.step: Season beef strips with salt and pepper, toss with flour. Melt 2 tbsp butter in a large pan over high heat. Sear beef quickly in batches — 1 min per side. Just brown the outside. Transfer to plate.
Add remaining butter. Cook mushrooms over high heat without stirring — 4 min until golden. Add onion 3 min, garlic 1 min.
Pour in broth, Worcestershire and Dijon, scraping up browned bits. Bring to a simmer.
Return beef to pan. Simmer on medium-low until beef is tender — 15–20 min.

Cook egg noodles in salted boiling water — 7 min. Drain. Remove stroganoff from heat. Stir in sour cream (don't boil after adding or it will split). Season generously. Serve over noodles with parsley.
Mix beef, pork, breadcrumbs soaked in milk, egg, onion, Parmesan, salt, pepper and 1 tsp Italian seasoning. Mix until just combined — don't overwork. Roll into golf ball-sized balls.
Heat olive oil in a large pan over medium-high. Brown meatballs on all sides — about 8 min. They don't need to be cooked through yet.
Add garlic to the pan — 1 min. Add crushed tomatoes, remaining Italian seasoning, sugar, salt and pepper. Bring to a simmer.
Return meatballs to the sauce. Cover and simmer on low until cooked through — 25 min.
Cook spaghetti in salted boiling water — 9 min. Drain. Serve meatballs and sauce over pasta. Finish with fresh basil and Parmesan.
Boil potatoes in salted water until completely tender — 20 min. Drain. Mash with butter and milk until smooth. Season with salt and pepper. Stir in half the cheese.
Preheat oven to 400°F. Brown ground beef with onion in olive oil over medium-high — 10 min. Add garlic 1 min.
Add flour, stir to coat the meat. Add tomato paste, Worcestershire and broth. Stir until saucy — 3 min. Add frozen veg. Season well.
Pour meat mixture into a 9×13 baking dish. Spread mashed potatoes evenly over the top. Rough up the surface with a fork. Sprinkle remaining cheese.
Bake until bubbling at the edges and top is golden — 25 min.
Preheat oven to 400°F. Pat salmon dry. Season with paprika, dill, salt and pepper.
For garlic bread: slice baguette, mix softened butter with 2 minced garlic cloves and parsley. Spread on bread, wrap in foil.
Place salmon on a baking sheet lined with foil. Drizzle with olive oil. Place garlic bread alongside. Bake both together — 12–15 min. Salmon is done when it flakes easily at the thickest part.
In a small pan, melt remaining butter with 2 garlic cloves over medium — 2 min until fragrant. Squeeze in lemon juice.
Plate salmon, spoon garlic butter over. Serve with garlic bread and chosen vegetable side.
Preheat oven to 425°F. Toss broccolini with olive oil, 3 minced garlic cloves, chili flakes, salt and pepper on a baking sheet. Roast until tender and slightly charred — 15 min.
Pat salmon dry. Season with salt and pepper.
Make garlic bread: mix butter with 2 minced garlic cloves. Spread on sliced baguette. Wrap in foil and add to the oven for the last 10 min.

In an oven-safe pan, heat olive oil over high. Place salmon skin-side up — cook 3 min until golden. Flip, transfer pan to the oven — 5–6 min. Salmon should flake but still be slightly pink in the centre.
Plate salmon with broccolini, a squeeze of lemon and garlic bread on the side.
Cook rice: rinse, combine with 2¼ cups water. Bring to boil, cover, simmer 15 min on lowest heat. Rest 5 min.
Whisk together soy sauce, mirin, brown sugar, garlic and sesame oil.
Pat salmon dry. Heat a non-stick pan over medium-high. Sear salmon flesh-side down — 3 min until golden. Flip, cook 2 min more. Skin side should be crispy.
Pour teriyaki sauce over salmon. Let it bubble and reduce, spooning over the fish — 2 min. Mix cornstarch with 1 tbsp water and add if you want a thicker glaze.
Serve over rice, topped with sesame seeds and green onions.

fridge.name: White fish fillets (tilapia, cod, or halibut)

recipe.step: Mix sour cream with juice of 1 lime, salt and a pinch of cumin to make the crema. Toss coleslaw mix with a squeeze of lime, salt and a drizzle of olive oil. Set both aside.
Cut fish into strips. Mix flour, cornmeal, cumin, paprika, garlic powder, salt and pepper in a shallow bowl. Dredge fish in the coating.
Heat olive oil in a pan over medium-high. Fry fish in batches — 3 min per side until golden and crispy. Don't move it while it's cooking. Drain on paper towels.
Warm tortillas in a dry pan 30 sec per side.
Build tacos: fish, coleslaw, a drizzle of lime crema, salsa and a squeeze of lime.
Cut potatoes into thick wedges (about ¾ inch). Soak in cold water 20 min to remove starch. Drain and pat very dry.
Heat oil in a deep pot to 325°F. Fry potato wedges in batches — 5–6 min until cooked through but not browned. Remove and drain. Increase oil to 375°F.
Mix 1 cup flour, baking powder, paprika and 1 tsp salt. Whisk in beer until smooth — batter should coat a spoon.
Return fries to 375°F oil — fry until deep golden and crispy — 3–4 min. Drain, season immediately with salt.

Dredge fish in plain flour, then dip in batter letting excess drip. Fry at 375°F — 4–5 min until batter is golden and crispy and fish flakes. Serve immediately with chips, malt vinegar and tartar sauce.

pantry.name: Canned tomato or butternut squash soup

recipe.step: Heat soup in a pot over medium, stirring occasionally — 8 min. Stir in a splash of cream or milk for richness. Season to taste. Keep warm on low.
Spread butter generously on one side of each slice of bread.
Heat a pan over medium. Place bread butter-side down. Lay cheese slices over. Top with second slice, butter-side up.
Cook until bottom is deep golden — 3–4 min. Flip carefully, press gently with a spatula. Cook until second side is golden and cheese is fully melted — 3 min.
Cut sandwiches diagonally. Serve with hot soup for dipping.
Place a baking sheet or pizza stone in the oven. Preheat to 475°F — at least 30 min for the pan to get blazing hot.
Divide dough into 2–3 portions. On a floured surface, stretch each to about ¼-inch thick. Don't use a rolling pin — stretch with your hands for a lighter crust.
Carefully transfer dough to hot pan (use cornmeal to prevent sticking). Spread sauce to the edges, leaving a 1-inch border.
Scatter cheese and toppings. Drizzle with olive oil, sprinkle Italian seasoning and garlic powder.
Bake until crust is golden and cheese is bubbling with brown spots — 10–12 min. Set up as DIY: put all the toppings on the table and let everyone build their own.
Soft-boil eggs: bring a pot of water to a rolling boil. Gently lower eggs in. Cook exactly 6.5 min. Transfer immediately to a bowl of ice water for 5 min. Peel carefully.
In a large pot, melt butter over medium. Add garlic and ginger — cook 2 min. Add miso paste, stir 1 min.
Pour in broth. Add soy sauce and mirin. Bring to a gentle simmer — 10 min. Taste and adjust seasoning.
Cook ramen noodles per package in a separate pot of boiling water — usually 3–4 min. Drain.
Divide noodles into bowls. Ladle hot broth over. Halve soft-boiled eggs and place on top. Add green onions, sesame oil, and any additional toppings.
Cook macaroni in salted boiling water until just al dente — 7 min. It will finish cooking in the sauce. Drain and set aside.
In a large pot over medium, melt butter. Add flour and cook, stirring — 2 min. It should look like wet sand.
Gradually whisk in milk and cream, a little at a time, until smooth. Bring to a gentle simmer, stirring constantly — 5 min until thickened.
Remove from heat. Add mustard, garlic powder, paprika. Stir in cheddar and Gruyère in handfuls until fully melted. Season with salt and white pepper.
Add macaroni to the sauce. Toss to coat. For a baked version: transfer to a baking dish, top with panko and a drizzle of butter, bake at 375°F for 20 min until golden.

pantry.name: Vegetable oil
pantry.qty: for frying (4 cups) or olive oil for oven fries

recipe.step: For frites: soak potato strips in cold water 30 min. Drain and pat completely dry. For oven frites: toss with oil, salt, spread on baking sheet, bake at 425°F 25 min flipping once.
Take steaks out of the fridge 30 min before cooking to come to room temperature. Pat completely dry. Season very generously with salt and pepper on both sides.
Heat a cast iron pan over HIGH heat until smoking — about 3 min. Add a thin film of oil.

Place steaks in pan. Don't move them. Cook 3–4 min per side for medium-rare (internal temp 130°F). In the last 2 min, add butter, garlic and thyme. Tilt the pan and baste the steaks with the foaming butter repeatedly.
Rest steaks on a board for 5 min — this is essential. Slice against the grain or serve whole with frites and Dijon.

fridge.name: Choice of protein (burgers, ribs, chicken, sausages)

pantry.name: Dry rub (paprika, garlic powder, salt, pepper, brown sugar)
Burger buns or hot dog buns

recipe.step: Preheat BBQ grill to medium-high — 10 min with lid closed.
Pat proteins dry. Apply dry rub generously and let sit at room temperature while grill heats.
Brush corn with butter and oil, season with salt. Grill corn, turning occasionally — 12–15 min until charred in spots.
Grill proteins, flipping once. Burgers: 4–5 min per side. Chicken thighs: 6–7 min per side. Sausages: 10–12 min, turning frequently. Brush with BBQ sauce in the last 3 min.
Rest meats 5 min before serving. Set up condiments and sides on the table.
Parboil diced potatoes in salted water — 8 min. They should be just tender but not falling apart. Drain and let them steam dry.
In a large cast iron pan over medium, cook bacon until crispy — 6 min. Remove with a slotted spoon, leave the fat in the pan.
Add potatoes to the bacon fat. Press down and don't stir — let them form a crust — 5 min. Flip sections and repeat — 5 min more.
Add butter, pepper, onion and garlic. Cook stirring occasionally — 5 min. Return bacon.
Make wells in the hash and crack eggs directly in. Cover and cook until whites are just set — 3–4 min. Season and serve straight from the pan with hot sauce.

fridge.name: Butter
fridge.qty: 3 tbsp, melted plus extra for the pan

recipe.step: Cook bacon in a pan over medium — 4 min per side until crispy. Or cook sausage patties 3–4 min per side. Keep warm in a 200°F oven.
Mix dry ingredients in a large bowl: flour, baking powder, sugar and salt.
Whisk wet ingredients in another bowl: eggs, milk, melted butter and vanilla. Pour wet into dry. Stir until just combined — lumps are fine. Don't overmix. Rest 5 min.
Heat a non-stick pan or griddle over medium. Add a little butter. Pour ¼ cup batter per pancake. Cook until bubbles form on the surface and edges look set — 2–3 min. Flip once, cook 1 min more.
Serve a stack with bacon or sausage and maple syrup.
Split and toast English muffins until golden — 3 min. Butter lightly.
In a large non-stick pan, melt 1 tbsp butter over medium. Add Canadian bacon — cook 2 min per side until warmed and slightly browned. Remove.

Add remaining butter. Crack eggs into round metal ring moulds if you have them for the classic shape (or just crack free-form into the pan). Season with salt and pepper. Cook 2–3 min until whites are set.
Add cheese slice to each egg. Cover with a lid 1 min to melt.
Layer muffin bottom → bacon → egg with cheese → muffin top. Serve immediately.

In a large heavy pot or Dutch oven, melt butter with olive oil over medium-low. Add onions and a big pinch of salt. Cook low and slow, stirring every 10 min — this takes 45–60 min to properly caramelize. They should be deep golden brown.
Add garlic, cook 2 min. Add flour, stir to coat — 2 min. Add wine, stir up any browned bits.
Add broth, thyme, bay leaves and Worcestershire. Bring to a simmer. Cook 20 min. Remove thyme and bay leaves. Season generously.
Toast baguette rounds under the broiler until golden — 2–3 min per side.
Ladle soup into oven-safe bowls. Float 2–3 baguette rounds on top. Pile Gruyère over. Broil 3–4 min until cheese is melted, golden and bubbling.
Heat olive oil in a large wide pan over medium. Cook onion until soft — 8 min. Add peppers — 5 min. Add garlic, cumin, paprika and chili flakes — 2 min.
Pour in crushed tomatoes and chickpeas (if using). Add sugar, salt and pepper. Simmer until sauce thickens — 15 min. Taste and adjust seasoning.
Use the back of a spoon to make wells in the sauce. Crack an egg into each well.
Cover the pan and cook on medium-low until whites are set but yolks are still runny — 5–7 min. Check often.
Crumble feta over the top if using. Serve straight from the pan with crusty bread for scooping.

pantry.name: Broccoli
pantry.qty: 2 cups (for those who want it on top)

recipe.step: Preheat oven to 400°F. Scrub potatoes clean. Prick each one 10–12 times with a fork all over.
Rub potatoes with olive oil and season generously with salt. Place directly on the oven rack.
Bake until a knife slides in with no resistance and skin is crispy — 60–75 min depending on size.
Steam or microwave broccoli until tender, if using.
Set up toppings family style: butter, sour cream, cheese, bacon, chives, broccoli. Let everyone load their own.

fridge.name: Shredded Mexican cheese blend or cheddar
Fillings of choice (cooked chicken, peppers, onion, black beans)

recipe.step: Prepare fillings: if using chicken, cook sliced chicken breast with cumin and seasoning — 5 min per side. Sauté any peppers and onions — 5 min.
Heat a dry pan over medium. Place one tortilla flat. Scatter a quarter of the cheese over half the tortilla. Add a quarter of the fillings. Fold tortilla over.
Cook until bottom is golden — 2–3 min. Flip carefully and cook the other side — 2 min. Cheese should be fully melted.
Repeat with remaining tortillas. Keep finished quesadillas warm in a 200°F oven.
Cut each into wedges. Serve with salsa, sour cream and guacamole.
Melt butter in a large pot over medium. Cook onion until soft — 5 min. Add garlic — 1 min.
Add carrots, celery, thyme and bay leaves. Cook 3 min.
Pour in chicken broth. Bring to a boil. Reduce to a simmer and cook until vegetables are just tender — 15 min.
Add egg noodles and shredded chicken. Cook until noodles are tender — 7–8 min.
Remove bay leaves. Season generously with salt and pepper. Ladle into deep bowls and garnish with parsley.
In a large pot, heat olive oil and butter over medium. Cook onion until very soft — 8 min. Add garlic — 2 min.
Add tomatoes, broth, basil and sugar. Bring to a simmer. Cook 20 min, breaking up tomatoes with a spoon.
Use an immersion blender to blend smooth — or transfer carefully to a blender. Stir in heavy cream. Season generously. Keep warm on low.
For grilled cheese: butter both sides of bread. Heat a pan over medium. Cook sandwiches until deep golden — 3–4 min per side, pressing gently. Cheese should be fully melted.
Cut sandwiches in half and serve alongside the hot bisque.
If making bacon or sausage, cook first: bacon 4 min per side, sausage 3–4 min per side until cooked through. Keep warm in the oven.
Whisk eggs, milk, cinnamon, sugar and vanilla in a shallow bowl until combined.
Melt 1 tbsp butter in a non-stick pan over medium. Dip bread slices in the egg mixture — 15 sec per side, letting it absorb but not get soggy.
Cook soaked bread in the buttered pan — 2–3 min per side until golden and slightly puffed. Work in batches, adding more butter as needed.
Dust with powdered sugar and serve with maple syrup and fresh berries.

fridge.name: Fillings: diced ham, cooked bacon, mushrooms, peppers, onion

recipe.step: Prepare all fillings first — dice everything into small pieces. If using mushrooms or peppers, sauté in butter 5 min. This is the prep station.
For each omelette: crack 3 eggs into a bowl. Add a pinch of salt and pepper. Beat with a fork until just combined — don't over-beat.

Melt 1 tbsp butter in a non-stick pan over medium-high until foamy. Pour in eggs. Let them set on the bottom for 30 sec, then use a spatula to push edges toward the centre while tilting the pan — 90 sec total.
When eggs are almost set but still slightly glossy on top, add fillings and cheese to one half. Fold the other half over.
Slide onto a plate. The residual heat finishes cooking the eggs. Repeat for each person. Set up all fillings on the table so people can choose.

fridge.name: Fillings: Nutella, jam, whipped cream, sliced strawberries

recipe.step: Blend flour, eggs, milk, 2 tbsp melted butter, sugar, salt and vanilla until completely smooth — or whisk vigorously by hand. Rest the batter in the fridge 30 min (this relaxes the gluten and improves texture).
Heat a non-stick pan or crepe pan over medium-high. Brush with a little butter.
Pour about ¼ cup batter into the pan and immediately swirl to coat the bottom in a thin, even layer.
Cook until the edges begin to look dry and the underside is lightly golden — 90 sec. Flip, cook 30 sec more.
Stack on a warm plate covered with foil. Repeat with remaining batter. Set up fillings on the table and let everyone fill and fold their own.
In a pan over high heat, brown ground beef with onion — 8 min. Drain fat. Add garlic — 1 min.
Transfer to slow cooker. Add crushed tomatoes, pasta sauce, carrot, Italian seasoning and milk. Stir.
Cook on LOW 6–8 hours or HIGH 3–4 hours.
Cook spaghetti in salted boiling water until al dente — 9 min. Reserve ½ cup pasta water before draining.
Toss pasta with sauce. Add pasta water to loosen if needed. Plate and finish generously with Parmesan.
In a pan over medium-high, brown chicken chunks in butter — 5 min. Not fully cooked, just browned.
Transfer to slow cooker with butter chicken sauce, tomatoes, onion, garlic, garam masala and yogurt. Stir.
Cook on LOW 6 hours or HIGH 3 hours until chicken is tender.
Stir in heavy cream. Cook uncovered on HIGH 15 min to thicken slightly.
Cook basmati rice with 2¼ cups water — bring to boil, cover, simmer 15 min. Warm naan in oven 3 min. Serve chicken over rice.
Mix smoked paprika, brown sugar, garlic powder, onion powder, cumin and salt into a rub. Pat pork dry and coat all over.
Place in slow cooker. Pour ½ cup BBQ sauce over. Cook LOW 8–10 hours or HIGH 5–6 hours until falling apart.
Shred pork with two forks — it should fall apart effortlessly. Discard any bones. Mix with remaining BBQ sauce.
Mix slaw: toss coleslaw with mayo, apple cider vinegar, salt, pepper and a pinch of sugar.
Toast buns in a dry pan 1 min. Load with pulled pork and a big scoop of slaw.
Place chicken in slow cooker. Whisk together honey, soy sauce, garlic and ketchup. Pour over chicken.
Cook on LOW 4–5 hours or HIGH 2–3 hours until chicken is cooked through and tender.
Remove chicken. Mix cornstarch with 2 tbsp cold water. Stir into the cooking liquid. Turn slow cooker to HIGH, cook uncovered 15 min until thickened.
Return chicken. Shred or leave whole.
Cook rice: 1½ cups rice with 2¼ cups water. Bring to boil, cover, simmer 15 min. Serve chicken over rice topped with green onions.

fridge.name: Beef stewing meat or chuck, cubed

recipe.step: Season beef with salt and pepper. Place in slow cooker with mushrooms, onion, garlic, broth, Worcestershire and Dijon.
Cook on LOW 8 hours or HIGH 4–5 hours until beef is very tender.
Mix cornstarch with 2 tbsp cold water. Stir into the slow cooker. Cook on HIGH uncovered 20 min to thicken.
Cook egg noodles in salted boiling water — 7 min. Drain.
Remove slow cooker from heat. Stir in sour cream (do not add while it's boiling or it will curdle). Season. Serve over egg noodles.
Mix beef, pork, breadcrumbs (soaked in milk first), egg, garlic, Parmesan, Italian seasoning, salt and pepper. Roll into balls the size of a golf ball.
Optional but recommended: brown meatballs in a pan with olive oil — 6 min, turning. Builds flavour.
Pour one jar of marinara into the slow cooker. Add meatballs. Pour second jar over top.
Cook on LOW 6–7 hours or HIGH 3–4 hours.
Cook spaghetti in salted boiling water — 9 min. Serve meatballs and sauce over pasta. Finish with Parmesan and fresh basil.
Boil potatoes in salted water until fully tender — 20 min. Drain, mash with butter and milk until smooth. Stir in half the cheese.
Preheat oven to 400°F. Brown beef and onion in a pan over medium-high — 10 min. Add garlic — 1 min.
Add flour, stir to coat. Add tomato paste, Worcestershire, broth and thyme. Cook until saucy — 3 min. Add frozen veg. Season well.
Pour filling into a 9×13 baking dish. Spread mashed potato evenly over the top. Rough up with a fork. Sprinkle remaining cheese.
Bake until bubbling at the edges and top is golden — 25–30 min.
Brown ground beef and onion in a pan over medium-high — 8 min. Add garlic — 1 min. Drain fat.
Transfer to slow cooker. Add all remaining ingredients except cornbread. Stir to combine.
Cook on LOW 6–8 hours or HIGH 3–4 hours. Taste and adjust seasoning.
About 30 min before serving: prepare and bake cornbread per package directions.
Serve chili with toppings on the side and warm cornbread.
In a large pan over medium-low, melt butter with olive oil. Cook onions very slowly until deep golden caramel — 35–40 min. Stir occasionally.
Add garlic — 2 min. Add wine, scrape up browned bits. Add broth, thyme and Worcestershire.
Season chicken with salt and pepper. Place in slow cooker. Pour onion mixture over.
Cook on LOW 6 hours or HIGH 3 hours until chicken is falling off the bone.
Transfer chicken to an oven-safe dish. Spoon onions over. Cover with Gruyère. Broil until cheese is melted and golden — 3–4 min. Serve with crusty bread to soak up the broth.
Cook macaroni in salted water for 3 min less than the package says — it will finish in the slow cooker. Drain.
In slow cooker, combine macaroni, butter, evaporated milk, whole milk, beaten eggs, mustard and garlic powder.
Add Velveeta and half the cheddar. Stir everything together.
Cook on LOW 2–3 hours, stirring every 45 min. Don't cook on HIGH or the eggs will curdle.
Add remaining cheddar in the last 15 min. Season with salt and white pepper. If it seems too thick, add a splash of warm milk.
Mix brown sugar, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, cumin, salt and pepper into a dry rub. Pat brisket dry and coat all over.
Optional: sear brisket in a hot pan with oil — 3 min per side for a better crust.
Place brisket in slow cooker. Pour broth and ½ cup BBQ sauce around the sides.
Cook on LOW 8–10 hours. Brisket should be very tender and easy to pull apart.
Slice against the grain or pull apart with forks. Toss with remaining BBQ sauce. Serve on toasted buns.
Cook bacon in a pan until crispy — 6 min. Drain, crumble and set aside. Reserve 2 tbsp bacon fat in the pan.
Cook onion in the bacon fat over medium — 5 min. Add garlic — 1 min. Add flour, stir — 2 min.
Transfer onion mixture to slow cooker with potatoes and broth. Season with salt and pepper.
Cook on LOW 6–8 hours or HIGH 3–4 hours until potatoes are very tender.
Use a potato masher to partially mash — leave some chunks for texture. Stir in cream, sour cream and most of the cheese. Taste and season. Serve topped with bacon, remaining cheese and green onions.
Season chicken with garlic powder, thyme, salt and pepper.
Combine broth, cream of chicken soup, onion and garlic in the slow cooker. Stir well.
Add chicken. Cook on LOW 5–6 hours or HIGH 2.5–3 hours.
Remove chicken, shred with forks. Stir in rice and return chicken. Cook on HIGH 20–30 min until rice is tender and has absorbed the liquid. Stir in frozen peas — they'll heat through in 5 min.
Stir in butter. Taste and adjust seasoning. Serve in deep bowls.
Get your campfire or camp stove grill going. You want medium-high heat — hold your hand 6 inches over the grate, you should be able to hold it there for about 3 seconds.
Form beef into 4 patties, slightly wider than the buns. Press a dimple in the centre. Season generously with salt and pepper.
Grill burgers 4–5 min per side, flipping once. Add cheese in the last minute. Hot dogs go alongside — grill 4–5 min turning often until blistered.
Wrap buns in foil with a tiny bit of butter. Set on the edge of the grill to warm — 3 min.
Set up the condiments station on the camp table. Load up.

fridge.name: Pie filling (apple, cherry) or pizza fillings

pantry.name: Canned diced tomatoes or salsa (for savoury)

recipe.step: Heat soup in a pot over the camp stove, stirring occasionally — 8 min. Season to taste.
Preheat your pie iron (jaffle iron) over the fire for 2 min. Butter both sides generously.
Build your pie: butter one side of two bread slices. Place one slice butter-side against the hot iron, add filling in the centre (don't overfill), top with second slice butter-side out.
Close the iron and hold over medium embers — 3–4 min per side until deep golden and sealed. Check often — they burn fast.
Let pies cool 1 min before opening — the filling is extremely hot. Build assembly-line style so everyone gets hot pies.
Get camp stove or grill up to medium heat. In a large cast iron pan, cook bacon until crispy — 6 min. Remove to a plate, keep the fat.
Add potatoes cut-side down to the bacon fat. Don't stir — press down and let them get a crust — 6 min. Flip and repeat.
Add butter, pepper, onion and garlic powder. Stir and cook 4 min until softened.
Push everything to the edges. Crack eggs directly into the centre. Cover loosely with foil and cook until whites are just set — 4 min.
Crumble bacon over everything. Season with salt and pepper. Serve straight from the pan with hot sauce.
Get camp stove to medium heat. Cook bacon until crispy — 4 min per side — or sausage 3–4 min per side. Keep warm wrapped in foil near the fire.
Mix pancake batter per package — most just-add-water mixes: 1 cup mix to ¾ cup water. For fluffier: use milk and eggs. Stir until just combined — lumps are fine.
Lightly grease pan or griddle with oil. Pour ¼ cup batter per pancake.
Cook until bubbles form on the surface and edges look set — 2–3 min. Flip once, cook 1–2 min more. Adjust heat as needed — camp stoves can be uneven.
Serve a stack with bacon and lots of maple syrup. The messier the better.
